summary:
The "Ending Improper Payments to Deceased People Act" (Public Law 119-77) amends Section 205(r) of the Social Security Act to improve coordination between the Social Security Administration, federal and state agencies, and the federal "Do Not Pay" working system, with the goal of reducing improper payments to deceased individuals.

The bill directs the Commissioner of Social Security to provide death information (collected under paragraph (1) of the same section) to the agency operating the Do Not Pay working system, "to the extent feasible." This data sharing is intended to help prevent improper payments and support the recovery of payments that were improperly made. The sharing must occur through a cooperative arrangement that meets existing statutory requirements (subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (3)). While data is being provided, the Commissioner and the Do Not Pay agency must enter into an agreement, based on an agreed-upon methodology, covering the proportional share of state death data costs; this agreement may be periodically reviewed.

The bill adds a new provision (paragraph (12)) barring the Commissioner from recording a death on a shareable record for any individual unless the Commissioner finds "clear and convincing evidence" supporting that the individual should be presumed deceased.

To address individuals who are incorrectly identified as deceased, the bill amends paragraph (7) to require the Commissioner, upon learning of such an error, to notify any agency that has a cooperative arrangement under paragraph (3) or (11) of the error.

These amendments take effect on December 27, 2026. The Act itself was approved on February 10, 2026.
